title: Von Thomas Next
description: A real catering operations and SEO-growth system with online booking, admin workflows, and city-service expansion planning.
body:
Von Thomas Next is a shipped operations platform for catering workflows.

It turns a messy service process into a web system: customers move through a clearer booking flow, the team gets admin visibility, and orders become easier to prepare without endless manual back-and-forth. The product sits close to the real business process: customer intent, menu choices, timing, communication, preparation, and operational handoff.

## What it supports

- Online catering configuration and booking flows for real customer requests.
- Admin workflows for day-to-day operational decisions.
- Inbox and communication visibility around customer requests.
- Order preparation, same-day edge cases, kitchen-ready handoff, and menu quantity logic.
- A public web surface that can grow through search, better landing pages, and clearer service positioning.

## Recent work from local commits

The last build days were broad and very operational. The offer generator gained better guest-count handling, variant limits for real guest counts, scalable buffet items, bottom actions, section CTAs, step-zero sales-page work, and improved autoscaling for package pricing.

The admin and operations side moved forward too: customer input audit logging, order audit events, customer confirmation checks, order-status handling, inbox rebuilds around Resend metadata, canonical inbox dates, explicit serving quantities, and safer quantity controls.

The menu/pricing layer saw V3 pricing profiles, proposal defaults, menu inventory allocation, Chef-Culinar auto-order work, package-alignment reports, sales-unit fixes, and concrete menu replacements such as the Kartoffelauflauf change.

The public-growth layer also moved: service page menu previews, sitemap generation for service/static/ratgeber/city routes, search-indexation fixes, ratgeber link treatment, a new offer-generator sales page, and Vercel geolocation for city personalization.

## What changed in the story

Von Thomas is now clearly more than a booking form. It is becoming an operating system for a real catering business: public demand capture, configurable offers, internal admin workflows, menu economics, email/inbox handling, and local SEO all have to fit together.

## Public boundary

This page describes product and operations architecture at a public level. It does not expose customer records, private inbox contents, supplier data, credentials, or internal financial details.
